evidence_quotes.quote: It is an unfair and deceptive practice to all consumers in this sector.
First off, it tricks consumer into thinking they own permanent product, but in fact is only a temporary product.

paragraphs.text: Can you please add protections to stop publishers from shutting off access to video games that they have already sold to consumers. It is an unfair and deceptive practice to all consumers in this sector. First off, it tricks consumer into thinking they own permanent product, but in fact is only a temporary product. In what other industry would this be permitted? Car companies cannot take away your vehicle once you purchase it, phone companies cannot take away your cellphone once you purchase it, so why can video game companies? The publisher should not be allowed to shut off access completely when there are many methods to preserving these games without destroying the company's bottom line. If publishers are allowed to continue this practice of destroying games, consumers will continue to be tricked into buying temporary titles, and it will lead to many gaming titles becoming lost art. There are methods to preserving these games without burdening publishers and consumers. Please consider this feedback, thank you.
